author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
ezPluginBundles can now configure their own data directories to use (#1985)

Moved plugin specific data into their own directories. The editor now automatically sets up data directories for the plugins that are selected.

Fixes #773.

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Data fixes (#1983)

* Fixed all assets
* Resaved most assets

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Added asset check panel (#1982)

Fixes #1846.

Added a "Check Assets" panel that allows you to run validation rules on all assets in the project.
This can be used to find errors in the data. The rules can theoretically be quite complex, though for now the built-in rules do very basic checks:
* check for unknown tags (fixes #1649)
* find empty objects
* find invalid required references (assets / files / strings)

Custom editor plugins can add custom validation rules as well.

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Introduced attribute to mark properties as required (#1981)

The asset browser widget and text widget now display a warning icon when a property is empty that is marked as required.

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Improved tag UI, data dir dialog, data fix (#1980)

* Changing data directory config now allows to restart editor automatically

* Fixed #1660: The reason was a badly rotated parent object.

The rotation was just so not normalized, which resulted in a slight de-sync between physics and rendering. Nothing that needs a code fix, just unfortunate scene setup.

* Improved tag UI widget

The tags UI widget now shows when an unknown tag is in use and allows to clear invalid tags.
Also added automatic selection refresh on config changes, to refresh the UI.

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Automatic LOD for terrain patches (#1979)

Adds a new CVar "Terrain.LodTargetCoverage" which specifies how detailed terrain patches should appear on screen.
Terrain patches that are more detailed than that, fade to LOD1 or LOD2.
At the edges, a "skirt region" is rendered (with some offset downwards. This is supposed to overlap with nearby terrain patches. When patches are at different densities (due to LOD or different resolutions), this skirt prevents cracks from showing up.
The LOD selection can be tweaked per patch to push it further out or pull it closer. LOD2 (1/16th triangle count) is the maximum LOD level, the skirt region isn't large enough for more reduction, and also the quality degrades too much after that.

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Fixed revert to default of exposed parameters (#1978)

* Fixed revert to default of exposed parameters

Fixes #1946
* Revert incorrect previous fix to crash when undoing prefab creation in `ezExposedParametersAsTypeDefaultStateProvider::GetDefaultValueInternal`. The problem was the the GUI was updating its state on deleted objects. Correct fix is to implement `ezQtExposedParametersPropertyWidget::DoPrepareToDie` correctly so that the child type widget is marked as dead.
* Fixed a small bug in `ezExposedParametersAsTypeDefaultStateProvider::GetDefaultValueInternal`: The super array was reduced when passing to the base class. However, the base class is still operating on the same level so the same provider so the same super array needs to be passed in.
* Added unit tests for prefab default state and undo / redo while selected. Added a new SphereTest prefab with exposed parameters for this purpose.

* Applied code formatting rules

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Added resource bindings to extractors (#1976)

* Added resource bindings to extractors

## RendererCore

- Added sampler, buffer and texture binding function to `ezExtractedRenderData` and `ezMsgExtractRenderData`, similiar to the `ezBindGroupBuilder` interface.
    - Bindings are applied to the `EZ_GAL_BIND_GROUP_FRAME` at the start of rendering.
    - Bindings are cached alongside dependencies for static objects.
- Moved clustered and simplified lighting GPU resource ownership and uploads into their extractors. The extractors now update GPU buffers with `ezGALDevice::UpdateBufferForNextFrame`, add their view dependencies and record the bindings needed by the render pipeline.
- Removed `ezClusteredDataProvider`, `ezSimplifiedDataProvider`, and the render pipeline pass provider binding/dependency hooks that are no longer needed.

## RendererFoundation

- Fixed Vulkan crash as `ezGALDevice::UpdateTextureQuality` was invalidating samplers used in bind groups. Fixed by invalidation all bind groups as well.
- Enabled `VK_KHR_GET_SURFACE_CAPABILITIES_2_EXTENSION_NAME` as it is a prerequisite for `VK_KHR_SURFACE_MAINTENANCE_1_EXTENSION_NAME`.

## Assets

- Updated the terrain heightfield test scene as the `HeightImageScale` property name had changed, causing test failures.

* Applied code formatting rules

* build fix

* remove ezMsgExtractRenderData binding support. Bindings should be handled by ezRenderer if needed.

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Simple console actions and generalized ImGui resources (#1969)

* Simple console actions and generalized ImGui resources

- Added `ezConsoleActions`, a central registry for input-backed console actions that keeps actions sorted by menu and action name, executes pressed shortcuts, and clears registered actions during shutdown.
    - Wired the default game application developer actions into `ezConsoleActions`, replacing the hard-coded per-action input handling path with a shared `HandleInput()` call.
    - Moved the ImGui console controls into a main menu bar with window toggles, layout reset, shortcut labels, and menu entries for registered console actions.
    - Added a new action to open the ezInspector with F10 key.
- Added support for registering custom ImGui console windows that can be toggled from the console menu and rendered while the console is open.
- Reworked Dear ImGui resource registration to use stable handle IDs backed by an ID table, with support for texture resources, raw GAL textures, and material resources. For this `ImTextureID` was changed to the handle size (uint32).

* Applied code formatting rules

* build fix

* clang tidy fix

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Render Graph Inspector (#1968)

* Render Graph Inspector

Introduces a live render graph inspection and texture preview system that
sends compiled render graph data from a running application to the Inspector
tool over telemetry, including per-pass resource access visualization and
interactive texture previews with histogram, min/max and per-pixel readback.

## RendererCore / RenderGraph

- Add `ezRenderGraphInspectionInfo`, `ezRenderGraphExecutionSummary`,
  `ezRenderGraphSwapChainSummary` and `ezRenderGraphInspectionSummary` as
  read-only snapshots of a compiled graph (passes, textures, buffers and
  flattened resource accesses), plus stream serialization operators
  (`RenderGraphInspectionInfo.h`, `RenderGraphSerialization.cpp`).
- Add `ezRenderGraphPassObserver`: observes a specific texture access within a
  pass by copying it into a persistent texture after the pass executes. Supports
  preview blit to a swap chain, sub-resource/channel/sample selection, value
  range clamping, zoom/pan, GPU histogram and min/max computation, and
  per-pixel readback.
- Extend `ezRenderGraphManager` with observer management and inspection queries:
  `GetExecutionSummary`, `GetRenderGraphInspectionInfo`, `CreateObserver`.

## RendererFoundation

- Add `ezGALTextureCreationDescription::GetNumberOfSlices` and `GetMipMapSize`. Reuse the latter from `ezGALTexture::GetMipMapSize`.
- Add `ezGALDevice::GetAllSwapChains` and make `GetBackBufferTextureFromSwapChain` const.

## InspectorPlugin

- Add `RenderGraphObserver.cpp` to broadcast execution summaries, inspection info and observer responses over telemetry, and to apply observer requests received from the Inspector tool.

## Inspector tool

- Add render graph UI: `RenderGraphWidget`, `RenderGraphOverviewWidget`, `RenderGraphPreviewWidget` and `RenderGraphHistogramWidget`, wired into the
  main window, actions and menus.

## Shaders

- Add compute/preview shaders for the observer: histogram clear/build, min/max clear/build, pixel readback and texture preview, with associated constant buffer and helper headers.

* Applied code formatting rules

* Fixes

* Applied code formatting rules

* clang tidy fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Added voxel functionality to Terrain System for volumetric editing (#1964)

Extends the terrain system by a "Terrain Volume" component.
This represents 3D space that is fully volumetrically editable using the existing terrain brushes.

This works basically the same as the heightmap based terrain patch component, but allows to create overhangs, caves and other complex geometry.

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Copy / Readback fixes (#1963)

* Copy / Readback fixes

## Renderer Changes

- Removed states from the render graph state machine that were only relevent under the assumption that we would cache graphs at some point.
- Fixed various bugs around sub-resources and block compression in texture upload / readback in `UpdateTextureForNextFrame` and Vulkan implementations.
- Fixed command encoder update semantics: buffer and texture updates perform their own upload work and no longer require the destination texture to already be in `CopyDestination` state.

## Tests
- Adds the `CopyUpdate` renderer test covering buffer copies, buffer updates, texture copies, texture region copies, texture updates, next-frame texture updates, array/cube/cube-array textures, NPOT textures, mip chains, padded source data, and BC1 textures.
- Adds the `IndirectDraw` renderer test covering indirect draw, indexed indirect draw, start instance handling, indirect argument offsets, and indirect dispatch.

* Applied code formatting rules

* clang tidy fixes

* build fix

* build fix

* reverting stuff, clang tidy broke build

* clang-tidy attempt 3

* Applied code formatting rules

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>

author: Jan Krassnigg
message:
Non-destructive Brush Based Terrain System (#1962)

Added a Terrain plugin which implements non-destructive terrain editing functionality.

The "terrain patch" component represents a heightfield, which can be modified by placing "terrain brushes".
These are objects with brush components attached, which define how to modify (raise, lower, paint) the terrain.
When moving a brush, the affected terrain patches are updated in real-time.

Patches can also be initialized from image data, to import height information from external sources.

Collision meshes are pre-baked on scene export, making the system compatible with the procedural placement system.

Terrain patches require dedicated shaders for rendering. The necessary infrastructure can easily be included in a custom shader. The system supports many different layers, since the shader simply uses a 2D array texture. The exact defails are up to the shader. On the heightfields themselves, up to 5 layers may be blended over a single vertex without artifacts (can be lowered to 4 or 3 to make the shader faster).

Brushes use "rounded rect" signed-distance fields, so they can be rectangular, circular and anything in between. They also allow to use noise. Brushes can be placed in prefabs to easily adjust terrain below an object (e.g. a building). When an object has both a brush and a spline component attached, the brush will follow the spline curve, to easily generate trails and mountains.

Functionality that's NOT available at the moment:
* LOD - all terrain patches are always rendered at full detail. May be added later.
* Erosion - not really suitable for this kind of system.
* Caves / overhangs - not possible with heightfields.
* Great terrain material shader - there is a lot that could be improved to make the terrain look better (noise, height base layer blending).

author: Sanakan8472
message:
Improved IPC connection state handling (#1960)

* Improved IPC connection state handling

## IPC Connections

* Made `ezIpcChannel::Connect()` report whether a connection attempt was actually started and move channels into `Connecting` state before the platform-specific connect work is queued. The state machine will then either transition to `Connected` or `Disconnected` after a timeout on a client and the server will stay in `Connecting` until a client connects.
* Added protocol message ids so the added IPC send/receive telemetry in `ezIpcProcessMessageProtocol` can be correlated across processes.
* Fixed Enet-based IPC channel and re-enabled testing.
* Reworked asset processor startup. Processor clients now start from a dedicated `StartClient` state which transitions to `WaitingForConnection` if the process could be started and eventually switches to `LookingForWork` if the IPC channel is connected.

## Linux

* Added Linux process group support using parent-death signals so launched child processes are terminated when the parent exits.
* Fixed deadlock when starting process as the old code did try to enter a lock in the allocator.
* Make POSIX process waiting more robust against interrupted waitpid calls and signaled exits. Mainly surfaced in `<defunct>` processes staying around.

* Applied code formatting rules

* build fixes

* Clang tidy

---------

Co-authored-by: ezEngine Automation <ezEngineProject@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Jan Krassnigg <jan@krassnigg.de>
